headline_summary: US-Israeli air campaign against Iran entered day six with intensified strikes reported in Tehran
Iran threatened retaliation beyond the Gulf after the US said it sank an Iranian warship off Sri Lanka
Iranian state TV was hacked to air opposition figure Reza Pahlavi, highlighting intensifying information operations
Strait of Hormuz disruption risk remains central for energy prices, tanker availability, and war-risk insurance premia
US military signaled strikes will expand deeper inland in Iran as Iran’s missile launch tempo reportedly declines
risk_drivers: Escalatory US/Israel strike tempo and stated intent to expand targets inland
Iran retaliation threats against Americans globally
Persistent shipping/insurance disruption risk around the Strait of Hormuz
Domestic instability in Iran after the killing of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and uncertain succession

Energy: Upside skew to crude and refined products on Hormuz disruption and tanker insurance constraints (oil_up_risk)
Higher freight and war-risk premia for tankers/LNG cargoes; potential rationing effects for import-dependent Asia
FX: Risk-off episodes likely to support USD and safe-haven FX; pressure on EM/high-beta FX (em_fx_pressure, risk_off_flows)
Rates: Flight-to-quality can pull core yields lower; oil shock raises inflation-risk and credit spread sensitivity (rates_credit_spreads)
Equities: Higher energy and shipping costs weigh on transport/consumer cyclicals; defense/energy may outperform

sources.title: Bombing of Tehran intensifies as war enters day six
US says Iran firing fewer missiles, US strikes to expand inland
